characters.name: Alice
characters.description: Really Sexy Aliceis the overall tritagonist ofSuperjail!. She's the hulking and muscular head prison guard ofSuperjailwho is transgender. She regularly engages in sadomasochistic rituals with the prisoners (often without their consent), though she rebuffsThe Warden's constant advances. Alice prior to living as a woman Alice is a tough and cruel person who rarely shows emotion and she usually punishes or tortures convicts for her own amusement. She seems to be attracted to muscular men ("Superbar") and displays clearhybristophilia, although this could be partially due to a lack of stable relationships that do not involve victimizing dangerous criminals and thus seeing this type of behavior as normal. (For example, in "Cold-Blooded," Alice thinks that the new prisoner is simply a quiet artist.) She also seems to show attraction towardsD. L. Diamond. She seems to feel the need to fulfill traditionally female roles, such as bridesmaid[2]or god mother[3], in addition to her established role as Corrections officer. Christy Karacas(Alice's voice actor) has confirmed that Alice is transgender, saying that she works atSuperjailbecause no other jail would hire her after coming out.

characters.name: Alien Fungus
characters.description: Superstorm!(cameo) Alien Fungus(no name given in show) (alternatively called theSpace FungusbyAliceor theExtraterrestrial FungusonThe Doctor's blackboard) is an extraterrestrial alien fungus who becomesJailbot's lover in theSeason 4episode "Jean, Paul, Beefy and Alice." The Alien Fungus was introduced byThe Twinsto be Jailbot's lover, but infects the entirety ofSuperjail. The Alien Fungus can infect its victims and turn them into Alien Fungus zombie monsters. The Fungus spreaded all over Superjail and infected almost all of the inmates, including the staff. The Fungus can even implant itself all over Superjail’s walls and outskirts. The Alien Fungus usually spreads its infection onto its victims by spraying them with green gas or goo, but can also spread trails and pieces of leftover Fungus onto them. There is no known confirmed cure for the Alien Fungus’ disease.

characters.name: Ash Firin
characters.description: Best Friends Forever(cameo)Mayhem DonorHot ChickGay WeddingThe Budding of the WarbuxxSuperjail Grand PrixVacationStingstressSuperfail!Uh Oh, It’s MagicSticky DischargeSpecial NeedsThe Trouble with TriplesNightshiftOedipus MessPlanet RadioBurn Stoolie BurnSuperhell!Last PackJean, Paul, Beefy and AliceThe Superjail InquisitorThe Superjail Six AshFirin(first said in "The Budding of the Warbuxx" byPaul, last name briefly visible in "Hot Chick") is a reoccurring inmate inSuperjail. Ash is a severely-burned pyromaniac with a strange power over fire, as seen in the episode "Mr. Grumpy Pants." He was working in theSuperjailblast-furnace when he happened upon alittle girlwhom he mistakenly thought was named "Cancer" (Sahn-surr), due to her medical wrist bracelet from a hospital for terminally-ill children. Ash is a severely-burned pyromaniac with a strange power over fire, as seen in the episode "Mr. Grumpy Pants." He was working in theSuperjailblast-furnace when he happened upon alittle girlwhom he mistakenly thought was named "Cancer" (Sahn-surr), due to her medical wrist bracelet from a hospital for terminally-ill children. It was revealed that Ash has a pathological fear of movies. It was in a movie theater that his father abandoned him, just prior to the fire that left Ash in his current state. Due to Ash being a Pyromaniac, he can assume to have been put in Superjail for arson. All deaths within the show that he caused (such as pushing amaninto a furnace andburning down the jail) have involved fire.

characters.name: Beefy
characters.description: "Sweet Cheeks Convict"Prisoner 33X91703  (Superbar) Hot ChickThe Budding of the Warbuxx(picture)VacationStingstressSuperfail!Sticky DischargeLast PackJean, Paul, Beefy and AliceThe Superjail Inquisitor(cameo) He seems to be a normal convict inSuperjail. However, he'd rather die than take body shots on Alice, so he decapitated himself with his own teeth. He was also kissed byHunterin "Hot Chick", but only because Hunter was looking for information onThe Twins. In the same episode, it is revealed that his relationship with Alice is completely one-sided. At the beginning of "Vacation", Beefy appears to be caged up in Alice's bedroom while being all terrified, obviously due to Alice's obsession with him.

characters.name: Bird
characters.description: SuperbarLadies Night(cameo)Don't Be A NegatonTerrorariumMr. Grumpy-PantsDream MachineTime-Police Part 1(Future verison)Time-Police Part 2Best Friends ForeverMayhem DonorLord Stingray Crash PartyHot ChickGhostsVacationStingstressSuperfail!Uh Oh, It’s MagicSticky DischargeSpecial NeedsThe Trouble with TriplesNightshiftPlanet RadioBurn Stoolie BurnSuperhell!Last PackThe Superjail Inquisitor Birdis an inmate inSuperjail. He is a small, yellow bird, who is very violent and dangerous. He's also a smoker and is most often seen sitting onGary's shoulder. Seems to be one of the smartest characters in the show, and usually he and Gary are behind the inside doings ofSuperjail. His intelligence is shown by the fact that Gary only has to make eye movement or face movement for Bird to understand any orders that are given. Bird might also be the reason why Gary is in jail, as Gary has never killed anyone. Bird is also quite brutal, as in one scene, Gary feeds him worms from his mouth. Gary's cellmate (Fatty) makes a joke by stating, "Hey Gary! I got a worm for you right here! I'm talking about my penis." In response bird flies over and rips Fatty's "penis" off screen. Bird and Gary then both chew on the penis. In the episode "Ladies Night", Bird also appears to be running a cult. characters.name: Black Face
characters.description: Black Faceis the undercover operation of the Stars N' Stripes army. He, along withPeepers,Blow Job,Rip TideandCook Out, were forced to stopLord Stingrayand his terrorist army. When the last remaining of the Stars N' Stripes spy onAlice, which they mistaken for one of Lord Stingray's henchmen, Black Face claims that they'll trace her and lead them to the whereabouts of Lord Stingray. However, Alice suddenly attacks the Stars N' Stripes members. Black Face tells them to attack Alice and also gunpoint's her so she can take them to her leader. Sometime later, Alice, Black Face, and the rest of the Stars N' Stripes burst through the wall and encounterThe Warden, Lord Stingray and the Inmates ofSuperjailin theoffice, while telling everyone to freeze. During the large fight betweenSuperjailand Lord Stingray'sarmy, Black Face is the only notable member to not be killed in an explosion, but was killed byMistress Killda, who was pulling her fingers in his head and was spinning it off. He is a African-American man that has black markings on his face. He also wears a military outfit, a gray helmet and scarf.

characters.name: Blow Job
characters.description: Blow Jobis the demolitions expert of the Stars N' Stripes army. Blow Job, along withPeepers,Rip Tide,Cook OutandBlack Face, were forced to stopLord Stingrayand his terrorist army. When the last remaining members of the Stars N' Stripes are attacked byAlice, who they were previously spying on, Blow Job was set on fire, from Cook Out's fire and Rip Tide's head, and explodes. He was then grabbed by Alice and smashed into Rip Tide by her before being tossed away. Sometime later, Alice, Blow Job and the rest of the Stars N' Stripes burst through the wall and encounterThe Warden, Lord Stingray and the Inmates ofSuperjailin theoffice. During the large fight betweenSuperjailand Lord Stingray'sarmy, Blow Job, along with Peepers, Rip Tide, and Cook Out ended up being killed in an explosion, with their severe heads landing in front of Alice. Blow Job wears a green military outfit, red stripes, a shield and other things. His eyes are never shown, except for once when he was killed. He also has a beard on his face.

characters.name: Bobo
characters.description: Time-Police Part 2Planet Radio(cameo)Superstorm!(cameo) Bobowas an inmate who grew to colossal size after he put his hand in a small puddle of growth serum spilled byJaredin "Terrorarium." He has the letters of his name, Bobo, which has been tattooed on each knuckle on his right hand. The only thing that he ever says is his own name ("Bobo"). He would later appear again in the chaotic part of the final battle during the end of "Time-Police Part 2" where he eats agiant mosquitoand one of the pirate skeletons from "Superbar" in one mouthful. Bobo appears again in "Planet Radio" where he is seen breaking an inmate out of a prison cell. He later appears again in "Superstorm!" where he won a tug of war at aSuperjailfield day event.

characters.name: Burning Convict
characters.description: Planet Radio(cameo) TheBurning Convict(no name given in show) is a convict who works withAshin the furnace/boiler room ofSuperjail. He wanted to burnCancerin theSeason 1episode "Mr. Grumpy-Pants." He has a pretty short moment in theshowbefore he is burned to death thanks to Ash (commonly nicknamed the Compassionate Convict). The Burning Convict makes a small cameo appearance in theSeason 3episode "Planet Radio." He once again appears in the furnace/boiler room, but with another inmate, listening to Planet Radio and laughing, while accidentally being set on fire. He is a very sadistic convict who only sees pleasure in the burning of others.
characters.personality_section: He is a very sadistic convict who only sees pleasure in the burning of others.

characters.name: Catastro
characters.description: Catastrois a large, bulky humanoid who isLord Stingray's assistant andMistress Killda's boyfriend. His face is concealed by a metal mask, which doesn't allow for the expressiveness that his basis had. We only really get to see his angry bulging eyes and a mouth of sharp teeth. He is mean and aggressive towards people that he doesn't know, mainly theSuperjailstaff. In fact, he almost grabbedThe Wardenwhen he tried to shake his hand. He was later killed when The Warden and the convicts smashed him with the head of The Warden's statue. He is mean and aggressive towards people that he doesn't know, mainly theSuperjailstaff. In fact, he almost grabbedThe Wardenwhen he tried to shake his hand. He was later killed when The Warden and the convicts smashed him with the head of The Warden's statue.

characters.personality_section: He is mean and aggressive towards people that he doesn't know, mainly theSuperjailstaff. In fact, he almost grabbedThe Wardenwhen he tried to shake his hand. He was later killed when The Warden and the convicts smashed him with the head of The Warden's statue.

characters.name: Charise
characters.description: Sally Donovan (season 2- present) VacationStingstressSticky DischargeSuperhell!Last Pack(appears in two photos)Superstorm! Charise[1]is the accountant forUltraprison, as well as the attendant forThe Mistress. She is the Ultraprison counterpart and girlfriend ofJared. As a person, Charise appears to be decent, rational, and even quite kind; this is a counter-balance to the cruel, indifferent, and unfaltering formal approach of The Mistress. The relationship between the two closely mirrors the relationship betweenJaredandThe Warden, with Charise acting as the mostly ignored "voice of reason" within the staff of Ultraprison in much the same way as Jared is withinSuperjail. It is not surprising that she and Jared become infatuated, and quickly fall in love. When she believed him to be dead, she got over him rather quickly and became infatuated withThe Doctormaking out with him constantly. Despite that, she possesses an innocent personality as she refused to sleep with The Doctor after he made several advances on her. This led to her dumping him and returning to Jared when the latter saves her from The Doctor's botched experiment though her non-sexual activities annoy him as well.

characters.name: Charlie Streyt
characters.description: Charlie StreytwasJean’s temporary new cellmate in theSeason 3episode "Sticky Discharge." Charlie arrives at Jean’s cell with a box of his possessions, says "howdy" to Jean, and calls him a friend. Jean heads to Charlie to ask about him. He gives Jean a paper and reveals that he is actually his brand new cellmate, now being transferred over toSuperjail. Jean gets angry and slaps the box of Charlie’s possessions out of his hands, upsetting him. The two then suddenly hearPaulwhispering outside their cell. As Paul is heading over to the cell, Jean tells Charlie to get over here to try and use him as the new love of his life so he can make Paul jealous. Jean kisses and makes out with Charlie while Paul just enters the cell room to move past the two and goes to the shelf to open it and throw out any of Jean’s crappy gifts to the ground. While Jean kisses and makes out with Charlie, Paul accidentally throws a sharp knife onto Jean’s shoulder to accidentally stab him, which stops his kissing and making out with Charlie in the process. Suddenly, the two seeThe Warden,JaredandJailbotarrive to the cellroom for an unscheduled contraband search. Jean and Charlie watch The Warden go over to them to discover the sharp knife in Jean’s shoulder and wonders about the knife’s possession. While The Warden assumes that the knife actually belongs to Paul’s, Jared tells him that they don’t always have to assume as they now have a third-party witness, which is Charlie. Unlike most inmates in Superjail, such as his temporary cellmate Jean, Charlie seems to be a very nice guy who is actually friendly towards others.
characters.personality_section: Unlike most inmates in Superjail, such as his temporary cellmate Jean, Charlie seems to be a very nice guy who is actually friendly towards others.

characters.name: Chris Fehn Inmate
characters.description: Chris Fehn Inmateis an inmate who appeared in "Cold-Blooded." He is an inmate wearing the mask of Chris Fehn. The inmate wears a standard orange/yellow prisoner uniform, but wears the mask of Chris Fehn, a former member of Slipknot.

characters.name: Chris Fehn Inmate
characters.description: Chris Fehn Inmateis an inmate who appeared in "Cold-Blooded." He is an inmate wearing the mask of Chris Fehn. The inmate wears a standard orange/yellow prisoner uniform, but wears the mask of Chris Fehn, a former member of Slipknot.

characters.name: Combaticus (character)
characters.description: Time-Police Part 2Hot Chick(cameo) Combaticusis a creation ofThe Twins. Combaticus seems to be brave, strong, and smart with a sense of responsibility. He protects The Twins in two episodes. The "son" of The Twins was created from their blood in "Combaticus" to irk The Warden. They generally treated him like a weapon earlier on, but later mourned his death in genuine sadness and gratitude near the end when he was crushed by a giant rock. The Twins released Combaticus's ashes from the urn into the air near a lake, and he presumably became the "new god of war." Presumed dead, he is not mentioned again until the end of "Time Police Part 2", when he returns to rescue The Twins, who cry with joy and hug him in an almost paternal sort of way. This was the only episode where Combaticus smiled. His only line was near the very end of "Combaticus", in which he said, "I am the new god of war. Bow down to Combaticus." He is a "clone", in a sense, of The Twins. He is nearly identical to them, with the exception of being noticeably smaller, having white skin and pure white eyes, and being covered in red and blue veins. He is, indeed, male, evident from the fact that one Twin uttered, "He only had one weakness, compassion." He, according to The Twins, had four hearts and fourteen fingers. He is the strongest thing you will ever see. Combaticus seems to be brave, strong, and smart with a sense of responsibility. He protects The Twins in two episodes. characters.name: Cook Out
characters.description: Cook Outis the flamethrower expert of the Stars N' Stripes army. Cook Out, along withPeppers,Blow Job,Rip TideandBlack Face, were forced to stopLord Stingrayand his terrorist army. However, Cook Out does accidentally kill and set the general of the Stars N' Stripes on-fire by his carefree usage of the flamethrower. In response, he apologies for his actions. When the last remaining members of the Stars N' Stripes are attacked byAlice, who they were previously spying on, Cook Out accidentally burns Rip Tide's head, which also hits Blow Job. Alice also grabs Cook Out and lifts him away. Sometime later, Alice, Cook Out, and the rest of the Stars N' Stripes burst through the wall and encounterThe Warden, Lord Stingray and the Inmates ofSuperjailin theoffice. When The Warden asks about them, Cook Out claimed that they're the good guys, which are with Superjail. During the large fight betweenSuperjailand Lord Stingray'sarmy, Cook Out, along Peepers, Blow Job and Ripe Tide, ended up being killed in an explosion, with their severe heads landing in front of Alice. Cook Out is a yellow-bearded cooker in a green, brown and red military outfit with stars on his shoulder pads, yellow glasses and a cowboy hat. He also wields a red flamethrower.

characters.name: Craig Jones Inmate
characters.description: Craig Jones Inmateis an inmate who wears a Craig Jones mask on his head. He was one ofJared's followers in theSeason 1episode "Cold-Blooded." The inmate was later mauled (and killed) by a polar bear. His most noticeable feature is his mask which is clearly based on Craig Jones from Slipknot, who wears the same mask, other than that his outfit is the standard orange/yellow prisoner uniform that every other inmate inSuperjailwears.

characters.name: Craig Jones Inmate

characters.name: Creepy Tattooed Man
characters.description: Creepy Tattooed Manis a tall and creepy inmate that is covered in tattoos who appeared only in "Lord Stingray Crash Party." His home isSuperjail, which was addressed byThe Wardenat the prison camp, along with theSerial RapistandWhite Supremacist Guy.

characters.name: Creepy Tattooed Man
characters.description: Creepy Tattooed Manis a tall and creepy inmate that is covered in tattoos who appeared only in "Lord Stingray Crash Party." His home isSuperjail, which was addressed byThe Wardenat the prison camp, along with theSerial RapistandWhite Supremacist Guy.

characters.name: D. L. Diamond
characters.description: Planet Radio(cameo) D. L. Diamond(full name:Darryl "The Dying" Diamond) isSuperjail's motivational speaker. In public, he pretends to be a laid-back, Michael Jackson-esque stage performer, yet in reality, Darryl "The Dying" Diamond is a horribly ravaged and unrefined criminal. Similar toJacknife, a criminal who is known for his share of committing crimes disgusting and sickening to other humans. Darryl's crimes are shown to be just as or if not worse than Jacknife's as his list of crimes include, but are not limited to: In public, he pretends to be a laid-back, Michael Jackson-esque stage performer, yet in reality, Darryl "The Dying" Diamond is a horribly ravaged and unrefined criminal. Similar toJacknife, a criminal who is known for his share of committing crimes disgusting and sickening to other humans. Darryl's crimes are shown to be just as or if not worse than Jacknife's as his list of crimes include, but are not limited to: When he was in danger he immediately told his back-up dancer Amber who he had just slept with in a calm and and non-caring attitude " If we make out of this Amber. I don't think it's going to work out between us", showing that he cares more for himself than others. Also, he is shown to be very greedy as when being held bythe Galactoidshe only offered a small percent of the money and profits he earned from Superjail and declared that he wouldn't go too far with that offer.  However, all that changed when he found that the aliens he thought were made-up were real and were exactly depicted as he said they were to the people of Superjail and he partied with them throughout their voyage.

characters.name: D.L. Diamond Spider
characters.description: The ugly spider about to eat Jared. D.L. Diamond's Spideris a giant, ugly, carnivorous spider created fromD.L. Diamond's "space dust". It's only appearance was in "Don't Be A Negaton." The creature has a face resembling that of D.L. Diamond.

characters.name: D.L. Diamond Spider
characters.description: The ugly spider about to eat Jared. D.L. Diamond's Spideris a giant, ugly, carnivorous spider created fromD.L. Diamond's "space dust". It's only appearance was in "Don't Be A Negaton." The creature has a face resembling that of D.L. Diamond.

characters.name: Donor Monster
characters.description: The Doctoramazed and in love with the Donor Monster. TheDonor Monsteris a living, destructive blob made up of blood, guts, organs, and limbs of convicts. It made its important appearance in "Mayhem Donor." The monster was created whenThe Twinsthrew an electric device at barrels full of the convicts' bodily things. The electricity brought the mess to life. Soon after, the Donor Monster findsThe Doctor, who becomes amazed and excited. The Doctor then becomes the "controller" of the monster by being inside it. The monster goes on a rampage, eating convicts/inmates and causing damage toSuperjail.

The monster goes on a rampage, eating convicts/inmates and causing damage toSuperjail.

characters.name: Double Rainbows Gang
characters.description: TheDouble Rainbowswere a (gay) gang that fought against thePurple Pythons(also a gay gang), and were led byPaul Guaye. The gang was dissolved in the episode "Gay Wedding" withJeanand Paul's eventual marriage. The outfits the gang members wore were jean jackets (usually with frayed cuts along the shoulders creating a vest like pattern) and their usual orange jump suits. Some gang members had multi-colored scarves and ascots. They all also had wrist bands that were also multicolored, which may indicate each gang members' rank.

characters.name: Fang
characters.description: StingstressBurn Stoolie BurnSuperhell!The Superjail Six FangisLord Stingray's pet. He is a baby Crynoc, an anglerfish-like creature. Fang is pale red with brighter and darker shade of red on his fins, lip, and tip of the lure. He has a big mouth full of jagged teeth, and a hooked lure on his head.

characters.name: Fang

characters.name: Fatty
characters.description: SuperbarCombaticusLadies NightDon't Be A NegatonTerrorariumDream Machine(cameo)Best Friends ForeverMayhem DonorLord Stingray Crash PartyHot Chick(cameo)GhostsJailbot 2.0The Budding of the WarbuxxSuperjail Grand PrixVacationStingstressUh Oh, It's MagicSticky DischargeSpecial NeedsThe Trouble with TriplesNightshiftPlanet RadioBurn Stoolie BurnSuperHell!Last PackJean, Paul, Beefy, and AliceThe Superjail InquisitorSuperstorm!The Superjail Six Fatty, often known as thePerverted Inmate, is a reoccurring inmate inSuperjail. Fatty is often very insulting and perverted, and is met with fatal repercussions in almost all of his appearances. A morbidly obese convict with a high pitched voice and a perverted attitude. Fatty arrived toSuperjailin the same batch of inmates asJared, which may suggest that he had some connection to the mafia in the outside world (in a previous scene a terrified Jared states that he has unknowingly worked for the mafia prior to his arrest). He is frequently seen massaging his own navel during his perverse outbursts. While Fatty is usually seen harassing fellow male inmates (sniffing his cellmate's underwear, offering to display his "family jewels", and so on), he does not hesitate to wolf-whistle at the attractive female inmates ofUltraprisona split second before a well-thrown high healed shoe punctures his eye and sends him over the railing. Although usually little more than an annoyance, Fatty displayed true aggression and blood-lust when forced to fight in the Pammelonian arena. Although, he was quickly defeated by a chainsaw-wielding opponent. Fatty is often very insulting and perverted, and is met with fatal repercussions in almost all of his appearances.
